result.markdown:
---
title: "Rate Changes in Event Series"
date: "2026-03-08"
categories: [R, ggplot, analysis, event series]
---

Event series are time series  that represent a number of events assigned to (equally-sized) time intervals. They serve as models for neuronal spike trains, for series of hydrogeological extreme events like Earth quakes or floods, or for the occurrences of ectopic heartbeats in a healthy heart rythm. 
Events series can have constant or time dependent event rates. How can this be distinguished?

Lets' consider an example with a constant rate.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(tidyverse)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
✔ ggplot2   4.0.0     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
✔ purrr     1.1.0     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
set.seed(1)
events <- rpois(10000,0.05)
event_series <- tibble(x = 1:length(events), y = events)
cols <- c("Events" = "black", "Spline" = "red", "Constant" = "blue")
events <- event_series %>% filter(y>0)
ggplot() +
     geom_rug(data = events, aes(x = x, col = "Events"), lwd = 0.05) +
     geom_smooth(data = event_series, aes(x = x, y = y, col = "Spline"), method = "loess", lty = "dashed", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.5) +
     geom_smooth(data = event_series, method = 'lm', formula = 'y ~ 1', aes(x = x, y = y, col = 'Constant'), se = F, linewidth = 0.5) +
     coord_cartesian(ylim= c( 0.02, 0.08)) +
     scale_color_manual(values = cols, breaks=c( 'Constant', 'Linear', 'Spline', 'Events')) +
     theme_minimal() +
     labs( x = "Time", 
       y = "Event Rate", 
       title = "Event Rates for a Poisson Process",  
       col = "Data/ Models", 
       caption = paste("Dr. Annette Witt\n Origami Data Science Services\n ",format(Sys.Date(), format = "%d. %B %Y"))) +
        
     theme(
        panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-1-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


The graph shows a rug plot of the events on the bottom and on the top the estimated time-dependent rate (red dahed line) with the condidence band (grey) and the estimaed constant rate (blue line).  The constant rate is very likely inside the 95%-condidence band of the time dependent rate. 
For event series with time-dependent rates, however, the following features are likely to be found:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(tidyverse)

len = 10000
rate = tibble(rate = 0.05 + 0.025* sin (0.0005*2*pi*(1:len)))
rate %>% mutate( x = row_number(), y = map_dbl( rate, function(x) rpois(1, x)) ) -> event_series
cols <- c("Events" = "black", "Spline" = "red", "Constant" = "blue")
events <- event_series %>% filter(y>0)

ggplot() +
     geom_rug(data = events, aes(x = x, col = "Events"), lwd = 0.05) +
     geom_smooth(data = event_series, aes(x = x, y = y, col = "Spline"), method = "loess",  span = 0.1, lty = "dashed", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.5) +
     geom_smooth(data = event_series, method = 'lm', formula = 'y ~ 1', aes(x = x, y = y, col = 'Constant'), se = F, linewidth = 0.5) +
     scale_color_manual(values = cols, breaks=c( 'Constant', 'Linear', 'Spline', 'Events')) +
     theme_minimal() +
     labs( x = "Time", 
       y = "Event Rate", 
       title = "Event Rates for a Poisson Process with time-dependent Rate",  
       col = "Data/ Models", 
       caption = paste("Dr. Annette Witt\n Origami Data Science Services\n ",format(Sys.Date(), format = "%d. %B %Y"))) +
        
     theme(
        panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr}

```
`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
```


:::

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-2-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


Here the constant rate (blue line) lies for many times outside the the confidence bad of the time dependent rate (grey area).
